name: Butler Matrix Array
description: A Butler matrix array is a beamforming network that uses a matrix of hybrid couplers and phase shifters to create multiple simultaneous beams from an array of antenna elements. The Butler matrix provides orthogonal beams with fixed beam directions, making it ideal for multi-beam applications. This passive beamforming network enables simultaneous reception or transmission in multiple directions without requiring active phase shifters.

num_elements.name: Number of Elements
num_elements.description: Number of antenna elements in the array, typically powers of 2

coupling_factor.name: Coupler Coupling Factor
coupling_factor.description: Coupling factor of the hybrid couplers in the Butler matrix

phase_shift.name: Progressive Phase Shift
phase_shift.description: Progressive phase shift between adjacent elements for beam steering

beam_width.name: Beam Width
beam_width.description: Half-power beam width of individual beams

designMethodology.overview: Butler matrix array design involves determining the matrix size, designing hybrid couplers and phase shifters, calculating beam directions, and optimizing the feed network for desired performance.

steps.title: Determine Matrix Size
steps.description: Select number of elements N (power of 2) and calculate required matrix size

steps.title: Calculate Beam Directions
steps.description: Determine beam pointing angles for each input port
steps.formula: theta_m = arcsin(m * lambda / (N * d)) where m = -(N-1)/2 to (N-1)/2

steps.title: Design Hybrid Couplers
steps.description: Design 3dB hybrid couplers with proper coupling and isolation

steps.title: Calculate Phase Shifts
steps.description: Determine required phase shifts for each path in the matrix
steps.formula: phi_mn = (2*pi/N) * m * n where m,n are port indices

steps.title: Design Phase Shifters
steps.description: Implement fixed phase shifters using transmission line lengths

steps.title: Optimize Feed Network
steps.description: Minimize insertion loss and maximize isolation between ports

steps.title: Verify Beam Orthogonality
steps.description: Ensure beams are orthogonal and have equal power distribution
steps.formula: Orthogonality = integral(E_m * E_n * dtheta) = 0 for m != n

equations.gain: G = 10 * log10(N * D * eta) where D is directivity and eta is efficiency
equations.radiationPattern: E(theta) = sum(I_n * exp(j * n * k * d * sin(theta) + j * phi_n)) for n = 1 to N

mockSolverHints.impedanceModel: Use scattering parameter matrix analysis for Butler matrix network with hybrid coupler and phase shifter models
mockSolverHints.radiationModel: Array factor multiplication with element pattern, considering mutual coupling effects between elements
keyAssumptions: ideal hybrid couplers with perfect isolation

references: Balanis, C.A. 'Antenna Theory: Analysis and Design', 4th Edition, Chapter 6
Stutzman, W.L. & Thiele, G.A. 'Antenna Theory and Design', 3rd Edition, Chapter 7
Volakis, J.L. 'Antenna Engineering Handbook', 4th Edition, Chapter 20
